questions: World Bank ar IMF er difference ki?
how does World Bank help developing countries?
answers: The World Bank is an international financial institution that provides loans and grants to developing countries for capital projects aimed at reducing poverty.
World Bank developing countries ke loan ar technical assistance dey infrastructure, education, health, ar poverty reduction er jonno.
Unlike the IMF which focuses on monetary stability, the World Bank specifically targets long-term economic development and poverty reduction through project financing.
The World Bank is funded by member country contributions and by issuing bonds in global markets, then channels these funds to development projects worldwide.

feelings.question: world bank er role ki?
feelings.answer: The World Bank's role in developing countries is to provide loans and grant funds for capital projects aimed at reducing poverty through various economic development initiatives.
